title: Dark Hormuz transits
summary:
Main
Dark Hormuz transits are rising as more Gulf flows go dark, reducing visibility on cargo origin, timing and availability. By Claire Jungman
Vortexa has tracked hundreds of dark transits through the Strait of Hormuz since March 1, representing 57% of all transits recorded over the period. This trend has only intensified through the crisis: the share of outbound laden vessels transiting dark stood at 58.5% in March, dipped to 54% in April as overall transit volumes peaked, and rose to 65.2% in May.

children.title: Main
children.summary:
Dark Hormuz transits are rising as more Gulf flows go dark, reducing visibility on cargo origin, timing and availability. By Claire Jungman
Vortexa has tracked hundreds of dark transits through the Strait of Hormuz since March 1, representing 57% of all transits recorded over the period.

children.title: Dark loads outside Hormuz are surging
section_path: Dark loads outside Hormuz are surging
children.section_path_text: Dark loads outside Hormuz are surging
children.summary: The Hormuz transit data captures only one channel. Vortexa-confirmed dark loading activity simultaneously increased sharply from April to May.

children.title: Inside Hormuz, the dark trend spans every major product class
section_path: Inside Hormuz, the dark trend spans every major product class
children.section_path_text: Inside Hormuz, the dark trend spans every major product class
children.summary: For vessels still transiting the Strait, crude and condensates remain the largest category of dark outbound laden movements, accounting for around 40% of the total. But the breadth of the trend is notable.

children.title: The emerging signal is non-Iranian participation
section_path: The emerging signal is non-Iranian participation
children.section_path_text: The emerging signal is non-Iranian participation
children.summary: The most important development is the changing operator profile. In March, non-Iranian operators accounted for 37% of dark outbound laden transits through the Strait.

children.title: Gulf flow visibility has materially deteriorated
section_path: Gulf flow visibility has materially deteriorated
children.section_path_text: Gulf flow visibility has materially deteriorated
children.summary: The practical consequence is that origin transparency for Gulf exports has deteriorated materially since March. Cargo that would previously have moved with a clear AIS trail from load port to discharge is now arriving with significant gaps in its journey - in some cases vessels are going dark at the Strait and resurfacing days or weeks later, thousands of miles away, with no intermediate signal.
